name: Faran Mine
description: Deep Helium-3 mine. Dancer's home. High mortality rate.
gm_instructions: Hotbed for Red radicalization. Heavy Copper magistrate oversight.

name: Agea
description: The sprawling capital city of Mars. Seat of ArchGovernor Nero.
gm_instructions: Heavily fortified. The Spire of Agea dominates the skyline.

name: The Spire
description: The 40km tall citadel of the Sovereign. Center of Society power.
gm_instructions: Entering requires a Gold-standard cover identity. Instant death if caught.

name: Deepgrave
description: Sub-oceanic prison for high-value political prisoners and rogue Obsidians.
gm_instructions: Almost impossible to escape. The pressure outside will crush a starShell.

name: Attica
description: Major Martian city controlled by House Bellona. Rival to Agea.
gm_instructions: Bellona stronghold. Constant skirmishes with Augustus client houses.

name: The Dragon's Maw
description: A brutal proving ground in the ice where Obsidians are trained and culled.
gm_instructions: Only the strongest survive to become Stained. Gold masters watch from heated bunkers.

name: Triton
description: Frozen moon, site of the Lovelock Engine and a massive prison colony.
gm_instructions: Where Fitchner was briefly exiled. Harsh, unforgiving environment.

name: Vesta Station
description: A massive hollowed-out asteroid serving as the primary transit hub of the Belt.
gm_instructions: Neutral ground. Smugglers, bounty hunters, and Syndicate bosses operate openly.

name: The Drift
description: Lawless territory of scattered asteroids. The Society does not patrol here.
gm_instructions: Perfect for Sons of Ares safehouses and Syndicate hidden caches.

name: Rhea (Ruins)
description: A moon burned to ash by Magnus au Grimmus as punishment for a Rim uprising.
gm_instructions: A chilling monument to Octavia's wrath. Highly radioactive.

name: Io (Raa's Seat)
description: Volcanic moon. The capital of the Rim Dominion. Honorable but strict.
gm_instructions: Rim Golds adhere tightly to martial honor codes. Outsiders are treated with intense suspicion.

name: Phobos Refinery
description: Quicksilver's massive Helium-3 processing hub.
gm_instructions: Secretly the financial nerve center for the Sons of Ares.

name: Ganymede Dockyards
description: The largest orbital construction yard in the outer planets.
gm_instructions: Currently building a classified Black-Flag stealth ship for the BoQC.

name: Whitehold (Ganymede)
description: The Board of Quality Control's local interrogation and genetic processing center.
gm_instructions: Where suspected Sons are taken. No one returns.

name: The Prefect's Hall
description: Opulent estate of ArchPrefect Syla. Features a massive dome overlooking Jupiter.
gm_instructions: Heavily guarded by elite Lurchers and Olympic Knight trainees.

name: The Blue Quarter
description: Strictly controlled district where Blue pilots live and manage orbital traffic.
gm_instructions: Blues govern themselves here. A great place to steal ship manifests.

name: The Gyre
description: A sprawling black market in the decommissioned sub-levels.
gm_instructions: You can buy anything here: weapons, forged sigils, information, or people.

name: Nagasos Exclusion Zone
description: A sealed mine where Fitchner crushed a Red uprising.
gm_instructions: The site of a terrible secret. Heavily monitored by the BoQC.

name: Doc's Corner
description: A small, poorly-lit medical clinic in the Underbelly run by a disgraced Yellow.
gm_instructions: A safe haven for wounded Sons operatives who can't go to official Yellow clinics.

name: The Spatter
description: A coolant-leak catchment basin in the Underbelly.
gm_instructions: Used as a covert meeting spot by the Sons. The noise masks conversations.

name: The Hanging Palace
description: A breathtaking, austere palace suspended over a frozen ocean, filled with marble columns and Ptolemaic effigies. It is the political heart of Ganymede.
gm_instructions: Emphasize the cold, silent atmosphere. The wait before meals is strictly observed here.

name: Dockyard 44 (The Scab)
description: A massive, rusted section of the 200km equatorial shipyards, notorious for illicit trades, missing shipments, and LowColor slums.
gm_instructions: This area is dangerous for HighColors unless heavily guarded. Perfect for Syndicate encounters.

name: The Deep Grave (Ganymede Branch)
description: A subterranean, claustrophobic labyrinth carved into the ice beneath the surface, serving as the Syndicate's local headquarters.
gm_instructions: Navigate using whispered passwords and Gray market tokens. High risk of Pink spies and Obsidian muscle.

name: The Garter Relay
description: A massive orbital mirror focusing sunlight onto Io's agricultural ring. Crucial for the Rim's survival.
gm_instructions: A key strategic point. Any sabotage here threatens the entire Rim with starvation.

name: Akari's Walk
description: A freezing, desolate pilgrimage path across the surface of Io leading to the Tomb of Akari.
gm_instructions: Used to test the endurance of Rim Golds. Extreme cold hazards apply.

name: Codovan Drydock Alpha
description: The pristine, heavily guarded facility where the largest Rim dreadnoughts are constructed in absolute secrecy.
gm_instructions: Access requires high-level Copper or Gold clearance. Patrolled by elite Grays.

name: The Copper Cauldron
description: A sprawling, toxic smelting facility where ores from the Belt are processed. The air is thick with smog and tension.
gm_instructions: Good location for finding corrupt administrators and overworked Reds.

name: The Whispering Galleries
description: A serene, acoustically perfect temple where the Rim's Whites adjudicate disputes and record history.
gm_instructions: A place of neutral ground, but everything said here is recorded and analyzed.

name: The Helium Drills (Trench 9)
description: Deep underwater mining facilities extracting helium-3. High pressure, dangerous, and isolated.
gm_instructions: Claustrophobic setting. The Reds here are hardy, rebellious, and highly suspicious of outsiders.

name: The Pink Labyrinth
description: A neon-lit, multi-level district offering every vice imaginable. Secretly a massive intelligence-gathering hub.
gm_instructions: NPCs here will try to extract secrets from players in exchange for favors or information.

name: Obsidian Encampment (The Stains)
description: A brutal, freezing camp where the Rim's Stained warriors train and live in primitive conditions away from the cities.
gm_instructions: Outsiders are challenged to combat. Respect is only earned through strength.

name: The Silver Exchange
description: A bustling, high-tech trading floor where the wealth of the Rim is managed. Contracts and debts are the only laws here.
gm_instructions: Useful for acquiring funds or tracking financial anomalies tied to Quicksilver.

name: The Rust Ward
description: A decaying medical and housing sector for LowColors. Overcrowded and prone to disease outbreaks.
gm_instructions: Players can find desperate allies or hire cheap muscle here.

name: The Sovereign's Embassy
description: A lavish, heavily fortified compound that stands out against the austere Rim architecture, housing Core diplomats.
gm_instructions: A den of spies. Players entering here are surveilled by both the Core and the Rim.

name: The Silent Ocean
description: The vast, frozen, opalescent sea of Ganymede. Beneath the ice lie ruins and secrets from before the Conquering.
gm_instructions: Hazardous environment. Potential location for hidden stashes or ancient technology.

name: Lykos Deep-Mine
description: A sprawling, brutal helium-3 mine where Reds toil under the false belief that they are pioneers preparing the planet.
gm_instructions: Highlight the oppressive heat, cramped conditions, omnipresent red dust, and the miners' ignorance of the surface world.

name: Agea
description: The capital city of Mars, a jewel of Gold architecture and power, ruled firmly by the Augustans.
gm_instructions: Describe towering spires, pristine streets reserved for HighColors, and the stark contrast with the smog-choked LowColor zones.

name: The Institute (Mars)
description: The brutal proving ground for young Golds, located in the Vallis Marineris, designed to cull the weak.
gm_instructions: Emphasize the artificial wilderness, the ruins of castles, and the ever-present floating cameras of the Proctors.

name: House Augustus Citadel
description: The impenetrable fortress of Nero au Augustus, overlooking Agea.
gm_instructions: Describe opulent wealth guarded by lethal, cutting-edge security and loyal Obsidian Praetorians.

name: Yorkton
description: A bustling industrial and administrative hub, slightly less prestigious than Agea but vital for planetary operations.
gm_instructions: Show the bureaucratic grind of Coppers and the industrial churn of Grays and Browns keeping the city running.

name: The Bleeding Place
description: A massive gladiatorial arena where slaves, beasts, and disgraced Colors fight to the death for entertainment.
gm_instructions: Focus on the smell of blood, the roar of the massive crowds, and the utter disregard for human life among the spectators.

name: Tinos
description: An ancient, abandoned terraforming colony deep underground, secretly repurposed as a rebel stronghold.
gm_instructions: Describe the echoing silence, archaic technology, and the palpable tension of a hidden rebellion preparing for war.

name: The Dragon's Tooth
description: A formidable military outpost and estate belonging to the Bellona, rivals of Augustus.
gm_instructions: Highlight martial discipline, statues of past Bellona heroes, and heavy armaments pointing towards Augustan territory.

name: Olympus (Mars Institute)
description: The floating command center above the Institute where Proctors feast and watch the students battle.
gm_instructions: Describe the decadent feasts, the holographic maps of the valley, and the arrogant aloofness of the Proctors.

name: Deepgrave
description: A maximum-security subterranean prison for political dissidents and dangerous criminals.
gm_instructions: Focus on sensory deprivation, brutal Gray guards, and inescapable isolation deep within the Martian crust.

name: Hyperion
description: The capital city of the Society, a sprawling, dazzling metropolis covering a massive portion of the moon's surface.
gm_instructions: Describe the dazzling artificial lights, endless traffic of sleek ships, and the oppressive weight of total Gold dominance.

name: The Citadel of Light
description: The seat of Octavia au Lune's power, a masterpiece of architectural intimidation and blinding white marble.
gm_instructions: Focus on the flawless surfaces, gold accents, and the terrifying, silent presence of the Olympic Knights on guard.

name: The Forum
description: The massive political arena where the Senate debates, though true power lies firmly with the Sovereign.
gm_instructions: Highlight the acoustics designed for booming speeches, opulent robes of the Senators, and the undercurrent of backstabbing.

name: The Dragon's Maw
description: The lavish Lunar estate of House Bellona, designed for grand galas and intense political maneuvering.
gm_instructions: Describe the extravagant gardens, whispered conversations in shadowed alcoves, and ostentatious displays of wealth.

name: The Plaka
description: A dense, labyrinthine district on Luna where Pinks, Browns, and Reds serve the whims and vices of the Core.
gm_instructions: Show the neon signs, the desperate hustle for survival, and the hidden dens of vice tucked away from Gold patrols.

name: The Morning Star Shipyards
description: The massive orbital shipyard where the Society's armada, including dreaded Moonbreakers, is constructed and maintained.
gm_instructions: Focus on the zero-G environment, the sparks of welders in the void, and the sheer, terrifying scale of the dreadnoughts.

name: The Sovereign's Menagerie
description: A private collection of exotic beasts and genetically modified creatures from across the solar system.
gm_instructions: Describe the beautiful but terrifying creatures, serving as a living metaphor for the Sovereign's control over nature itself.

name: Institute of the Violet Muses
description: An elite academy where Violets are trained to create art, music, and shape flesh for the Golds' entertainment.
gm_instructions: Highlight the surreal art pieces, the eccentric and demanding instructors, and the underlying cruelty of their creativity.

name: The Ash Lord's Spire
description: The imposing residence of Magnus au Grimmus; dark, austere, and heavily fortified against all intrusion.
gm_instructions: Describe the faint scent of ash, the stark obsidian decor, and the chilling, disciplined silence of his elite guards.

name: The Pandemonium Club
description: An exclusive, highly illegal underground club where bored Golds indulge in the most depraved and extreme vices.
gm_instructions: Focus on masked patrons, sensory overload, and the total suspension of standard Society rules and morality.

name: The Drowned City of New York
description: The ruins of old Earth's metropolis, partially submerged but repurposed for high-society retreats and exclusive parties.
gm_instructions: Describe the juxtaposition of ancient, decayed skyscrapers with sleek, modern Gold yachts floating smoothly between them.

name: The Amazonia Preserves
description: Massive, artificially maintained jungles kept strictly for hunting excursions by Peerless Scarred.
gm_instructions: Highlight the humid, dangerous environment, genetically restored predators, and the thrill of the hunt among the ruins of nature.

name: The Marianas Trench Prison
description: An inescapable black-site prison located at the bottom of the ocean, reserved for the Sovereign's most hated enemies.
gm_instructions: Focus on the crushing pressure outside, the dim bioluminescent lighting, and the absolute hopelessness of the inmates.

name: The Himalayan Monasteries
description: Secluded, high-altitude retreats where White Justicars and philosophers debate Society doctrine and dispense absolute justice.
gm_instructions: Describe the thin air, the stark white architecture blending with snow, and the rhythmic chanting of logic-mantras.

name: The Ruins of London
description: An irradiated or simply abandoned zone, left as a stark monument to Earth's past failures before the Conquering.
gm_instructions: Focus on the crumbling monuments, the gray, ash-choked skies, and the desperate scavenging low-colors eking out a living.

name: Pacifica Hub
description: A massive floating city serving as the primary navigation and piloting academy for Earth-born Blues.
gm_instructions: Describe the sterile, screen-filled environments, the constant hum of servers, and the algorithmic, emotionless logic of its inhabitants.

name: The Sahara Glass Fields
description: Areas of the desert turned to glass by orbital bombardment during the Conquering, now a stark, beautiful, and deadly monument.
gm_instructions: Highlight the blinding reflection of the sun, the razor-sharp terrain, and the intense, unforgiving heat.

name: The Alps Chalets
description: Ultra-exclusive winter resorts for Core Golds to ski, socialize, and scheme away from the politics of Luna.
gm_instructions: Focus on the pristine snow, roaring fireplaces, and the casual cruelty of the elite guests towards their shivering servants.

name: The Congo Bio-Labs
description: Sprawling, highly classified research facilities where Yellows develop new medicines and horrific biological weapons.
gm_instructions: Describe the sterile white halls, unsettling biological specimens floating in vats, and the pungent smell of antiseptic.

name: The Great Wall Promenade
description: The ancient structure reinforced and turned into a massive, planet-spanning walkway for parades and military displays.
gm_instructions: Focus on the sheer scale of the wall, the massive banners of the great Houses, and the rhythmic stomping of Gray legions.

name: The Floating City of Aphrodite
description: A massive, opulent city suspended in the upper atmosphere of Venus, safe from the acidic, crushing surface below.
gm_instructions: Describe the shimmering forcefields, endless, decadent parties, and the air heavy with intoxicating perfume and scheming.

name: The Sunken Forges
description: Deep-atmosphere industrial platforms where Oranges build intricate machinery under extreme pressure and heat.
gm_instructions: Highlight the cramped, sweltering conditions, the frantic genius of the Orange engineers, and the constant threat of hull breach.

name: The Rose Gardens of Carthii
description: Vast, gravity-defying botanical gardens showcasing the most beautiful and deadly engineered flora in the system.
gm_instructions: Describe the intoxicating scents, vibrant colors, and the hidden toxic thorns of the carefully cultivated plants.

name: The Venusian Institute
description: An Institute known for its focus on naval tactics, political maneuvering, and poison over pure brute strength.
gm_instructions: Focus on the treacherous floating islands, the emphasis on fragile alliances, and the constant, paranoia-inducing threat of betrayal.

name: The Aerian Docks
description: A massive sky-port where sleek Venusian yachts and deadly strike cruisers are moored against the violent winds.
gm_instructions: Describe the elegant design of the ships, the crisp efficiency of the Blue crews, and the howling winds outside the hangar shields.

name: The Palace of Mirrors
description: A labyrinthine estate where walls are made of reflective materials, confusing enemies and amplifying light and vanity.
gm_instructions: Highlight the disorienting visuals, the constant paranoia of being watched from every angle, and the sheer vanity of its inhabitants.

name: The Acid Wastes
description: The harsh, crushing, highly acidic surface of Venus, used to dispose of the worst traitors and failed experiments.
gm_instructions: Describe the corrosive yellow clouds, the unimaginable crushing pressure, and the absolute finality of being sent there to die.

name: The Silk District
description: The premier training and modification center for Pinks destined for the Core's most elite and demanding masters.
gm_instructions: Focus on the tragic beauty of the inhabitants, the sterile surgical modification bays, and the deep emotional conditioning.

name: The Cloud-Seas
description: Vast expanses of thick, navigable clouds where wealthy Golds race skiffs and engage in highly illegal aerial duels.
gm_instructions: Describe the rushing wind, the sheer thrill of speed, and the deadly, unseen drops waiting if a ship's engine fails.

name: The Whispering Towers
description: Communication arrays and intelligence hubs managed by Whites and Coppers to monitor communications across the Core.
gm_instructions: Highlight the ceaseless stream of data holograms, the cold calculation of the analysts, and the immense power of hoarded information.

name: The Dragon's Maw
description: A massive, hidden shipyard deep within the volcanic crust of Io, producing illegal Dreadnoughts for the Rim's secret fleet.
gm_instructions: Players discovering this can upset the balance of power between Core and Rim. High security, Obsidian guards.

name: Sulfur Springs Retreat
description: A luxurious, sulfur-scented bathhouse and diplomatic meeting ground where Rim Golds negotiate away from Core spies.
gm_instructions: Good place for eavesdropping or political assassination. Weapons strictly forbidden.

name: The Ashfall Pits
description: A brutal gladiator arena run by Syndicate bosses where low-colors fight for survival and betting.
gm_instructions: Players can enter to win information or rescue a captive.

name: Helios Observatory
description: An ancient observatory perched on a volcanic peak, used for deep-space tracking and intercepting Core communications.
gm_instructions: Hackable terminal with major secrets.

name: Loki's Forge
description: A neutral-ground black market built into a dormant volcano where illegal weapons from the Belt are traded.
gm_instructions: Excellent for acquiring heavy ordinance or rare tech.

name: The Obsidian Barracks
description: The main training ground for the specialized Stained Obsidians loyal to the Rim.
gm_instructions: Extreme danger; combat encounters here are lethal.

name: The Ilium Shipyards
description: The largest legitimate shipyard in the Rim, though currently functioning under Core quotas. A bustling hub of engineering.
gm_instructions: Industrial espionage is rampant here. Good for sabotage missions.

name: Echo Dome
description: An abandoned terraforming dome now used as a massive, sprawling slum and Syndicate stronghold.
gm_instructions: Dangerous for high-colors. High density of cutthroats and smugglers.

name: The Sapphire Gardens
description: Romulus au Raa's private, serene rock gardens used for meditation and private audiences.
gm_instructions: Requires high social standing to enter. A place for crucial dialogue.

name: Ganymede Dock 44
description: A heavily corrupted shipping dock where undocumented cargo and human trafficking slip past Rim authorities.
gm_instructions: Investigation here reveals Syndicate ties to local Golds.

name: The Ice Quarries
description: Brutal low-color labor camps harvesting deep ice. Tensions are incredibly high, nearing revolt.
gm_instructions: Inciting a riot here can create a massive distraction.

name: The Sunken Spire
description: A pre-Conquering ruin submerged beneath Ganymede's ice crust, rumored to hold ancient tech.
gm_instructions: Dungeon crawl environment. Watch for automated defenses.

name: The Deep City
description: A submerged metropolis beneath Europa's ice, relying on geothermal vents. A marvel of Rim engineering.
gm_instructions: Submarine travel required. High pressure environments.

name: Leviathan's Graveyard
description: A dark, abyssal trench where ancient derelict ships from the Conquering were dumped.
gm_instructions: Good for salvaging, but dangerous genetically modified aquatic fauna lurk.

name: The Crystal Palace
description: An opulent casino and resort catering to Core Golds visiting the Rim, acting as a honeypot for Rim spies.
gm_instructions: Perfect for social encounters, heist scenarios, or gathering intel on Core VIPs.

name: Siren Station
description: An underwater listening post tapping into the Rim's submerged fiber-optic networks.
gm_instructions: Hacking this gives access to major Rim communications.

name: The Drowned Market
description: A nomadic fleet of submarines trading illegal goods in the dark oceans of Europa.
gm_instructions: Location changes weekly. Requires a guide to find.

name: Poseidon's Breath
description: A massive geothermal energy plant that powers much of the Deep City.
gm_instructions: Sabotaging this plunges the region into darkness and cold.

name: Dead Man's Drop
description: A notorious cliffside settlement known for tossing traitors into the icy abyss. A haven for bounty hunters.
gm_instructions: Hostile environment. Players will be challenged to duels or ambushed.

name: The Callisto Penal Colony
description: A high-security prison for political dissidents and captured Core spies.
gm_instructions: Jailbreak scenario. High security, automated turrets.

name: Stargazer Keep
description: A small, heavily fortified outpost maintained by House Bellona as a listening post against the Rim.
gm_instructions: High tension area. Good for instigating conflict between Core and Rim.

name: The Silent Craters
description: A vast, irradiated wasteland from past orbital bombardments. Scavengers sometimes brave it for scrap.
gm_instructions: Environmental hazards like radiation. Hidden caches of pre-rebellion gear.

name: Valhalla Dome
description: An abandoned outpost on the far side of Callisto, said to be a staging ground for deep-space raiders.
gm_instructions: Creepy, survival-horror atmosphere. Evidence of brutal raids.

name: Eros Station
description: A massive, hollowed-out asteroid serving as the capital of vice, gambling, and black-market trade in the Belt.
gm_instructions: Anything can be bought here. High risk of being scammed or robbed.

name: The Rusty Nail
description: A rundown but heavily shielded cantina on a mining asteroid, favored by independent pilots and smugglers.
gm_instructions: Great place to hire a pilot or pick up rumors.

name: Ceres Citadel
description: The primary garrison for the Society's armada in the Belt, maintaining order over the miners.
gm_instructions: Heavy military presence. Avoid unless equipped for a full-scale assault.

name: The Graveyard of Ships
description: A dense cluster of destroyed vessels from various skirmishes, extremely hazardous to navigate.
gm_instructions: Piloting checks required. Good salvage, but pirate ambushes are common.

name: Tartarus Mine
description: A brutal, high-yield Helium-3 mine worked by Red slaves in horrendous conditions.
gm_instructions: Good target for early rebellion or humanitarian sabotage.

name: The Hidden Forge
description: An illegal, mobile manufacturing plant hidden within a hollowed asteroid, making unregistered weapons.
gm_instructions: Players can disrupt the Syndicate's weapon supply here.

name: Outpost 31
description: A derelict research station studying anomalous asteroid compositions, abandoned after a mysterious incident.
gm_instructions: Contains experimental tech but automated security is hostile.

name: The Spider's Web
description: A heavily encrypted communication hub hidden in a hollow asteroid, used by Pink information brokers.
gm_instructions: Crucial for tapping into the Syndicate's spy network.

name: Pallas Refinery
description: A massive ore processing facility. It is heavily automated and crucial to the local economy.
gm_instructions: Sabotage here ripples across the Belt's economy.

name: The Wounded Lion
description: A heavily modified, immobile dreadnought serving as a pirate haven and chop shop.
gm_instructions: Good for modifying ships off the books, but dangerous.

name: Vesta Shipyards
description: A smaller, corporate-run shipyard for repairing Core patrol vessels.
gm_instructions: Good place to steal a ship or plant a tracker.

name: The Void Dancer's Rest
description: A rumored, perfectly spherical asteroid containing an ancient, pre-color garden and vault.
gm_instructions: Mythic location. High reward, ancient traps.

name: The Crimson Tear
description: A hidden cell of early rebels plotting supply chain disruptions, operating out of an abandoned mine.
gm_instructions: Potential allies, but highly paranoid and likely to shoot first.

name: Juno's Eye
description: A long-range sensor array hidden on a fast-moving asteroid, spying on Rim fleet movements.
gm_instructions: Hacking it provides intel on both Core and Rim fleets.

name: The Slag Heaps
description: A massive orbital dump where refuse from Ceres is launched. Inhabited by desperate outcasts.
gm_instructions: Disease and starvation are rampant. Hidden valuables in the trash.

name: The Obsidian Crucible
description: A secret proving ground where Rim Obsidians are pitted against captured beasts in zero-G combat.
gm_instructions: Brutal spectacle. Can be used to prove one's worth to Rim Golds.

name: Deep Space Relay Station Alpha
description: The furthest comms relay connecting the Rim to the deep dark of the Oort Cloud.
gm_instructions: Very lonely outpost. Often targeted by deep-space raiders.

name: The Weeping Wall
description: A secluded, moisture-streaked retaining wall in the lower levels of Hyperion, often used for clandestine meetings by Pink and Brown servants.
gm_instructions: Use for covert information hand-offs or discovering hidden rebel caches.

name: Sector 4G Gamma Pit
description: An abandoned, highly irradiated side-shaft in the Lykos mines where the Sons once tested rudimentary explosives.
gm_instructions: Describe the faint glow and the crackle of a geiger counter; good for finding old schematics.

name: Aphros Orbital Weeping Glass
description: A series of small, private orbital viewing platforms that catch the upper atmospheric acid storms, creating a beautiful weeping effect on the glass.
gm_instructions: Ideal for a tense negotiation with a mid-tier Gold or an assassination attempt.

name: Europa's Rib
description: The remnants of a pre-Conquering skyscraper jutting out of the irradiated Earth landscape, used as a temporary roost by Obsidians on hunting rites.
gm_instructions: Introduce aggressive wildlife or leftover pre-Conquering tech.

name: The Boiler Room
description: A grimy, boiling-hot pub built into the geothermal venting system of lower Agea, frequented by Red smugglers.
gm_instructions: A place to hire off-the-books pilots or buy black-market stims.

name: Vault of the Ancestors
description: A heavily guarded, rarely visited sub-basement archive containing original treaties from the Conquering.
gm_instructions: High-security infiltration mission; players might find secrets about the original Iron Golds.

name: The Glass Garden
description: A private botanical garden owned by House Saud, featuring carnivorous flora genetically engineered to consume pests.
gm_instructions: Can be used as a death trap or a location to find rare, deadly poisons.

name: Helium-3 Depot 99
description: A decommissioned refueling station on the Martian moon, slowly degrading and overlooked by patrols.
gm_instructions: Perfect for a temporary rebel base or a place to hide a stolen ship.

name: The Silent Forum
description: An acoustic-dampening chamber originally designed for meditative retreats, now used for highly sensitive political plotting by young Peerless.
gm_instructions: Eavesdropping here requires advanced tech; great for uncovering political plots.

name: Deep-Sea Trench 4
description: An underwater research facility studying mutated aquatic life in the post-nuclear oceans of Earth.
gm_instructions: Claustrophobic horror setting; potential for acquiring unique biological assets.

name: Hephaestus Scrap Yard
description: A scrap metal yard where illegal modifications to low-tier armor and weapons take place.
gm_instructions: Players can upgrade gear here but risk drawing Syndicate attention.

name: The Echoing Halls of Octavia
description: A natural cavern system on Venus whose acoustics carry whispers for miles, used by spies to pass messages anonymously.
gm_instructions: Characters must decipher overlapping whispers to find the real intel.

name: The Sunken Vault
description: Pre-Conquering ruins submerged off the coast, rumored to hold uncorrupted data drives from before the Golds took over.
gm_instructions: Requires specialized diving gear; guarded by automated pre-Conquering defense systems.

name: Sector 12 Slag Heaps
description: Massive mountains of industrial waste where scavengers pick through the refuse for scrap metal and discarded tech.
gm_instructions: Good place for a gritty ambush or meeting a low-level informant.

name: The Gilded Cage
description: A luxurious, inescapable penthouse used to comfortably imprison high-value political hostages.
gm_instructions: Infiltration requires bypassing high-end bio-scanners and Gold-tier security.

name: The Ash Pits
description: A gladiatorial training ground where low-colors fight in volcanic ash for the amusement of minor Golds.
gm_instructions: Players can earn credits or reputation through brutal pit fighting.

name: The Blind Oracle
description: A secluded orbital satellite above Mars used by House Augustus for secure, off-grid communications.
gm_instructions: Hacking this satellite reveals troop movements and secret directives.

name: The Neon Alley
description: A narrow, vibrantly lit street in the commercial district famous for illegal cybernetic enhancements and black-market drugs.
gm_instructions: Players can acquire illegal tech or run into Pink syndicates here.

name: The Siberian Wastes
description: A vast, frozen expanse used by Obsidian hunting parties to test their endurance and track mutated beasts.
gm_instructions: Hostile environment; survival skills are paramount. Great for earning Obsidian respect.

name: The Obsidian Spire
description: A jagged, black rock formation serving as a proving ground for young Obsidians before they are sent to the academies.
gm_instructions: Players can witness brutal rites or attempt to recruit exiled Obsidians.

name: The Psychedelic Canopy
description: A dense, genetically modified jungle where the trees secrete hallucinogenic sap, confusing navigation.
gm_instructions: Use hallucination mechanics; characters might face their worst fears or uncover hidden rebel camps.

name: The Manhattan Skeletons
description: Massive, crumbling skyscrapers spanning a radioactive river, often used by scavengers and desperate refugees.
gm_instructions: Sniper alley; a great location for a tense standoff or a treacherous crossing.

name: Mars Munitorum 7
description: An underground weapons factory producing standard-issue gear for Grays, heavily guarded and highly efficient.
gm_instructions: Sabotage objective; players can disrupt supply lines or steal prototype weapons.

name: The Icarus Club
description: An exclusive zero-gravity club for young, wealthy Golds to socialize and consume designer narcotics.
gm_instructions: High-society infiltration; excellent for gathering blackmail material or assassinating a target.

name: Lykos Deep Sector 14
description: A completely automated, abandoned mining sector where the Sons hide their most sensitive data and operatives.
gm_instructions: Filled with deadly automated defense systems and environmental hazards.

name: The Acid Pools
description: Naturally occurring pools of highly corrosive acid used for industrial processing and, occasionally, disposing of bodies.
gm_instructions: Dangerous environmental hazard; players can find evidence of murders or hidden caches nearby.

name: Poseidon's Tomb
description: A deep-sea base established by early Gold conquerors, now flooded and populated by mutated aquatic life.
gm_instructions: Extreme pressure environment; requires specialized subs and suits. High risk, high reward.

name: The Iron Wastes
description: A vast desert of rust and jagged metal, leftover from terraforming failures, scoured by intense sandstorms.
gm_instructions: Survival challenge; navigation is nearly impossible without specialized gear. Hides old tech.

name: The Shadowed Halls
description: A sprawling network of ancient tunnels beneath the surface, used by smugglers and thieves to move undetected.
gm_instructions: Players can hire guides, buy contraband, or get ambushed in the dark.

name: The Diamond Catacombs
description: Stunning, naturally glowing crystal caverns occasionally visited by eccentric Golds for inspiration.
gm_instructions: Beautiful but deadly; the crystals can shatter and cause severe lacerations. Potential for finding rare gems.

name: The Irradiated Zone
description: A heavily radioactive area untouched since the Conquering, rumored to hold ancient, dangerous technology.
gm_instructions: Lethal radiation mechanics; players need heavy shielding to explore. High chance of finding pre-fall tech.

name: The Red Dust Tavern
description: A relatively upscale bar for Grays and Browns, serving synthetic alcohol and heavily monitored by local security.
gm_instructions: Good place to overhear rumors or recruit low-level mercenaries. Watch out for informants.

name: The Floating Gardens
description: A series of interconnected, zero-g biomes suspended above the city, maintained by a small army of Greens and Browns.
gm_instructions: Stunning visuals; players can find rare botanical ingredients or assassinate a target in plain sight.

name: The Boiling Sea
description: A massive body of near-boiling water, home to specialized, heat-resistant flora and fauna.
gm_instructions: Dangerous environment; boats require heavy shielding. Good for hiding underwater bases.

name: Geneva Vault 4
description: An ancient, sealed bunker complex from the pre-Conquering era, its automated defenses still active and lethal.
gm_instructions: Requires hacking and combat skills to penetrate. Holds significant historical data or weapons.

name: The Scrap Market
description: A bustling, chaotic market where scavengers sell everything from broken tech to illegal weapons.
gm_instructions: Players can find almost anything here for the right price, but risk getting scammed or robbed.

name: Far Side Relay Station
description: A secluded, automated observation post monitoring deep space, rarely visited by maintenance crews.
gm_instructions: Excellent location for a covert meeting or to slice into the Society's communication network.

name: The Venomous Jungle
description: A dense, genetically modified jungle where every plant and animal is highly venomous.
gm_instructions: Lethal environment; players need antidotes and careful navigation. Hides secret biological research facilities.

name: The Broken Spire
description: The remains of a massive pre-Conquering tower, now home to a large colony of mutated, aggressive avian creatures.
gm_instructions: Vertical exploration challenge; players can find old tech in the upper levels but must fight off the birds.

name: The Old Draft Grounds
description: The ruins of an old Institute proving ground, occasionally used by nostalgic or sadistic Golds for illegal death matches.
gm_instructions: High danger; players might be forced to fight or can find leftover Institute gear.

name: Ilium Black Market
description: A sprawling, lawless bazaar hidden within a decommissioned terraforming plant beneath the ice.
gm_instructions: Watch for Syndicate enforcers demanding heavy tolls. Good place for illegal tech.

name: Station 44-Delta
description: An isolated Red worker hab carved into a nickel-iron asteroid. Conditions are brutal.
gm_instructions: High chance of encountering radicalized Sons of Ares operatives plotting sabotage.

name: The Weeping Dock
description: A massive, secret drydock where experimental rim stealth ships are built.
gm_instructions: Heavily patrolled. Any intrusion results in immediate lethal force.

name: Kuiper Relay Alpha
description: Deep-space communication relay masquerading as a rogue comet.
gm_instructions: Can be hacked to intercept secure Gold transmissions from the Core.

name: Europa's Tear
description: A submerged listening post beneath miles of ice, accessible only by specialized submersibles.
gm_instructions: Requires underwater gear. The water pressure is deadly if the hull breaches.

name: The Rust Yards
description: Ship graveyard used by scavengers and pirates to strip down stolen Core vessels.
gm_instructions: Hazardous environment. Watch for unstable plasma cores and desperate scavengers.

name: Outpost Echo
description: Forward observation post monitoring ship traffic near the Jovian borders.
gm_instructions: Garrisoned by elite Obsidian Stained. Avoid direct confrontation.

name: Pallas Deep Mine
description: A high-yield helium-3 mine worked entirely by low-Colors under harsh conditions.
gm_instructions: The workers are on the brink of revolt. Players can tip the scales.

name: The Shadowed Spire
description: A lavish estate belonging to a disgraced Rim Gold, isolated on a frozen plateau.
gm_instructions: Contains valuable political secrets but is guarded by advanced automated defenses.

name: Ceres Black Docks
description: Hidden resupply point for smugglers moving goods between the Core and the Rim.
gm_instructions: Excellent place to refuel off the grid, but trust no one.

name: The Silent Forge
description: Underground weapons manufacturing facility specializing in railguns.
gm_instructions: Loud, hot, and dangerous. Sabotaging it could cripple local Rim forces.

name: Callisto Processing Plant
description: Massive water purification plant supplying several nearby moons.
gm_instructions: Crucial infrastructure. Heavily guarded by Grays.

name: The Howling Void
description: A region with intense magnetic interference where many ships have mysteriously vanished.
gm_instructions: Comms will go down. Good place to hide, but navigational hazards are extreme.

name: Station Kilo-9
description: A supply cache hidden inside a hollowed-out asteroid.
gm_instructions: Requires a specific transponder code to enter without triggering the self-destruct.

name: The Gilded Cage
description: A luxurious but heavily guarded prison for high-value political hostages.
gm_instructions: Infiltrating requires immaculate disguises and forged credentials.

name: The Methane Seas
description: Sprawling extraction rigs harvesting hydrocarbons for fuel.
gm_instructions: Hazardous explosive gases everywhere. No energy weapons allowed.

name: The Chasm Market
description: A black market clinging to the walls of a massive ice fissure.
gm_instructions: Vertical navigation required. Slippery and dangerous.

name: The Ashen Wastes
description: Volcanic plains littered with the ruins of a failed terraforming experiment.
gm_instructions: Toxic atmosphere. Good place to lose pursuers, but requires environmental suits.

name: Orbital Relay 7
description: Key communication hub connecting the Rim moons.
gm_instructions: Hacking this relay provides real-time fleet movements for the sector.

name: The Crimson Deep
description: A hidden underwater Red settlement that escaped Gold rule.
gm_instructions: Extremely xenophobic to outsiders. Players must prove their loyalty to the Rising.

name: Vesta Shipyards
description: Massive orbital construction facility building heavy cruisers.
gm_instructions: A prime target for sabotage or theft of a major vessel.

name: The Obsidian Pens
description: Training and holding facility for Rim-bred Obsidians.
gm_instructions: High concentration of deadly warriors. Do not provoke them.

name: The Hollowed Moonlet
description: A pirate haven built inside a small captured moonlet.
gm_instructions: Rowdy and dangerous. Violence is common. Good for hiring mercenaries.

name: The Frozen Spire
description: A research facility studying ancient, pre-Conquering tech found deep in the ice.
gm_instructions: High-tech security. Contains valuable data but also unknown hazards.

name: Sector 4 Quarantine
description: An abandoned mining colony overrun by a genetically engineered plague.
gm_instructions: Biohazard suits mandatory. The infected are hostile and mutated.

name: The Icarus Station
description: A solar observatory dangerously close to the sun's influence, monitoring solar flares.
gm_instructions: Constant risk of solar radiation. Good place to hide from thermal sensors.

name: The Sulfur Mines
description: Brutal penal colony where prisoners mine sulfur by hand.
gm_instructions: Constant volcanic activity makes the environment unpredictable and deadly.

name: The Whispering Caves
description: A network of ice caves used as a rendezvous point for rebel leaders.
gm_instructions: The acoustics are strange; whispers carry for miles. Sneaking is impossible.

name: The Scrap Citadel
description: A massive fortress made entirely of welded-together derelict ships.
gm_instructions: The local warlord demands tribute from anyone passing through.

name: Outpost Omicorn
description: A small, isolated listening post monitoring the deep ocean below.
gm_instructions: Staffed by paranoid Grays. Good source of intel on what lies beneath.

name: The Scorched Earth
description: A region bombarded by orbital strikes during the Dark Rebellion. Now a wasteland.
gm_instructions: Radiation is high. Scavengers occasionally find valuable tech here.

name: The Ice Palace
description: The opulent seat of power for the ruling families of Ganymede.
gm_instructions: Security is absolute. Access requires royal invitation or incredible stealth.

name: The Void Market
description: A nomadic fleet of ships that forms a temporary black market before jumping away.
gm_instructions: Finding it requires deciphering coded broadcasts on the dark net.

name: Sector 7 Mining Rig
description: A standard automated mining rig with a minimal crew.
gm_instructions: Easy target for piracy, but the distress beacon will summon rapid response.

name: The Sunken City
description: Ruins of an early colonization attempt that sank beneath the ice.
gm_instructions: Unstable structures and freezing water. Contains lost historical data.

name: The Fire Pits
description: Geothermal power plants supplying energy to the orbital shipyards.
gm_instructions: Intense heat and toxic fumes. Sabotaging them will plunge the sector into darkness.

name: The Hidden Dock
description: A small, concealed dock used for smuggling operatives into the capital.
gm_instructions: Guarded by loyalist Reds. Very useful for inserting a strike team.

name: The Debris Field
description: A massive field of wreckage from a past space battle.
gm_instructions: Navigation is treacherous. Many unexploded ordinances and valuable scrap.

name: The Crystal Caves
description: A natural formation used as a high-security vault for the Rim's wealthiest.
gm_instructions: Protected by state-of-the-art laser grids and automated turrets.

name: The Ash Market
description: A trading post located near active volcanoes, dealing in illegal weapons and slaves.
gm_instructions: The air is choked with ash. A grim and brutal place.
